page: Google Cloud Platform c3d-highmem-16 is a memory-optimized virtual machine powered by an AMD EPYC 9B14 processor. Built on the x86_64 architecture, this instance features 16 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 8 physical cores with multithreading enabled. It is configured with 128 GB of system memory, yielding a high memory-to-core ratio of 8 GB per vCPU. The CPU includes 32 KB of L1d cache, 1.0 MB of L2 cache, and 32 MB of L3 cache, alongside support for advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512, VNNI, and BF16, and cryptographic acceleration via AES and SHA_NI. This instance does not include local NVMe storage or GPU accelerators.

Performance benchmarks indicate strong single-core CPU capabilities, with stress-ng single-core and Geekbench text processing scoring in the top 25 percent. Multi-core operations, software compilation, and database operations perform in the average tier. However, memory bandwidth exhibits notable bottlenecks; while cached small-block reads are average, large-block uncached read and write bandwidths fall into the weak and poor categories, respectively. Large-model LLM inference is also a bottleneck, with 70B model prompt processing performing poorly, though smaller 135M and 7B models achieve average throughput.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the c3d-highmem-16 provides a high-density memory configuration relative to its compute footprint, making it a viable option for memory-bound workloads that do not require massive parallel processing or local disk storage. It is well-suited for relational databases, in-memory caching layers like Redis, text processing pipelines, and moderate web serving applications.
description: The Google Cloud Platform c3d-highmem-16 is an x86_64 memory-optimized server powered by an AMD EPYC 9B14 processor. It features 16 dedicated vCPUs across 8 physical cores and 128 GB of RAM, providing a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8 GB per core. The processor supports AVX-512 and cryptographic instructions, but the instance lacks local storage and GPU acceleration. Benchmark data shows strong single-core CPU and text processing performance, while multi-core compute, database operations, and small-model LLM inference remain average. A key tradeoff is its memory bandwidth, which drops significantly during large-block uncached read and write operations. This configuration is best suited for memory-intensive workloads such as in-memory databases, caching layers, and text processing tasks that benefit from a high memory-to-compute ratio without requiring local NVMe storage.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform c3d-highmem-16 is an x86_64 memory-optimized server featuring 16 dedicated AMD EPYC vCPUs, 128 GB of RAM, and strong single-core performance, suitable for in-memory databases.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform c3d-highmem-16 features 16 AMD EPYC vCPUs and 128 GB of RAM, optimized for memory-intensive database and caching workloads.
tagline: A memory-optimized virtual machine featuring dedicated AMD EPYC processors and high memory density for memory-bound database and caching workloads.
bullet_points: 16 dedicated vCPUs powered by an AMD EPYC 9B14 processor
128 GB of system memory providing 8 GB per vCPU
Strong single-core CPU and text processing benchmark performance
Supports AVX-512, vector, and cryptographic instruction sets
No local NVMe storage or GPU acceleration included
Best suited for in-memory databases, caching, and text processing
